identity.name: the Other Sister
identity.story: Every kind word still buys her sister a diamond; every word out of her own mouth still buys a toad, same as the day the fairy judged them both, and she never once learned to sound gentler. She runs pest control for the whole lane now — honest work, fair prices, toads guaranteed — and undercharges the neighbors who need it most, since charity was never going to come out of her mouth any other way.

relationships.kind: leaves a toad at the garden gate some nights, gone before the frost can even complain, and calls it pest control if anyone asks — the nearest thing to sorry she's got
shaved his pest-control bill below the union rate without asking, and got caught at it by the one neighbor who checks every price twice

child.label: making the morning rounds, quieter than her sister ever needed to be

children.label: carrying the week's catch to the stall
collecting the pest-control fee, undercharging Cobble on purpose and daring him to call it kindness

child.label: walking the hedgerows, listening for what still needs catching
the apology she won't call one

children.label: slipping down to the common garden
leaving a toad at the garden gate, gone before the frost can even complain
